pattern: Track only the endpoints of the string built so far, because every future join only ever touches the two ends -- collapsing an exponential sequence of left/right choices down to a DP over a small (first letter, last letter) state space.
hints: At each step you choose to attach the next word on the left or the right of what you've built so far -- but nothing about the middle of that running string matters for any join that comes after it, only its first and last character.

Keep a map from (first character, last character) to the shortest length achieving it after processing the first several words; a new word can only ever produce a handful of new (first,last) states from each existing one.

For every current (first,last,length) state and the next word, try both attachment orders; when the shared boundary letters match, the join deletes one character, so subtract one from the combined length before recording the new state.

constraints.note: each word length 1..50, lowercase English letters only

edge_inputs.description: two words with no overlap either order
chain of words that all share boundary letters both ways
three words, deletions possible in more than one order
words at the max length of 50, repeated letter
greedy left-to-right joining is not optimal here
no pair of adjacent letters ever matches, so length only ever grows

stress.description: 1000 random lowercase words of varying length up to 50
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    n = max(1, size)
    words = []
    for _ in range(n):
        length = rng.randint(1, 50)
        words.append(''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(length)))
    return [words]
